description: StandardDnsResolver and ConfigServiceResolver imported in network_provider.py lines 9-10 but never instantiated or injected. build_session_router() passes channel_lookup=lambda _: None instead of a real resolver. Service discovery is ineffective — all endpoint resolution falls back to None/empty.
proposed_solution: Instantiate resolver in assembler or factory; inject into SessionRouterAdapter; modify select() to call resolver when cache is empty

description: build_circuit_breaker() defined in network_provider.py:57-69 creates a working AsyncCircuitBreaker but assemble() never calls it. AsyncChannelPool has no circuit-breaker slot. YAML circuit_breaker.enabled: true is never read.
proposed_solution: Wire CircuitBreaker into AsyncChannelPool (accept optional cb param in __init__); integrate allow_request/record_success/record_failure into channel lifecycle

description: assemble() builds components linearly — if component 5 fails, components 1-4 are wasted. No pre-flight check of config completeness or dependency availability. No wiring/validator.py exists.
proposed_solution: Create wiring/validator.py with validate_graph(AppConfig) returning List[ValidationError]; call at top of assemble() before any build call

description: 5 factory modules (network, transport, session, presentation, protocol) produce objects but no test assembles them into a full pipeline and exercises FabricClient.send/receive roundtrip. Composition failures may go undetected until production.
proposed_solution: Create integration test that (a) builds full assembler with test config, (b) wires FabricClient, (c) does send/receive roundtrip; run in CI on every PR

description: No definition of ComponentNode, dependency edges, or visual/CLI dependency graph. Onboarding requires reading every factory and assembler manually. No wiring/graph.py exists.
proposed_solution: Create wiring/graph.py with ComponentNode dataclass and dependency edges; add optional --graph CLI flag to main.py

description: Neither wiring/ nor wiring/adapters/ contains __init__.py. Breaks static analysis, IDE autocompletion, and some packaging tools like setuptools autodiscovery.
proposed_solution: Add empty __init__.py to wiring/ and wiring/adapters/
